name: Swadraj Beverage Distribution ERP
description:
1. Business Context

Company Overview

Name: Swadraj Agencies

Type: FMCG (Fast-Moving Consumer Goods) Distribution

Service Area: Pimpri-Chinchwad, Maharashtra

Scale: 2 Warehouses, 3 delivery vehicles, 6-8 staff

Hours: 8 AM – 8 PM, 7 days a week

Products:


Bailey Water
Parle Agro Products (Frooty, Appy Fizz, B-Fizz, Smoodh)
Bisleri & Reliance Mineral Water
Local Mineral Water (Masti, Sahyadri, Oxyterra, Oxygreth)


Product Range: 60 ml to 5 liters (various packaging formats: PET bottles, Tetra packs)

Customer Segments: Retailers, Distributors, Wholesalers, Walk-in customers

Payment Modes: Cash, UPI, Online, Cheque, Credit (outstanding balance)

Operational Structure

The business operates as a three-part workflow:


Office: Data management, orders, billing, ledger, payments
Warehouse: Inventory management, stock loading/unloading
Service/Logistics: Vehicle-based sales, delivery, collection, field reporting


Operational Scope


Daily transactions: Multiple orders from outlets, bulk orders, last-minute walk-in orders
Vehicle fleet: 3 named vehicles (Rajgad, Sinhgad, Purandar) operating simultaneously
Transaction types: Sales orders, invoices, cash/cheque/online payments, credit tracking, returns/adjustments
Critical pain point: Coordination between office planning, warehouse loading, and vehicle field operations; daily reconciliation of cash, stock, and invoices



2. User Roles & Responsibilities

Office Manager / Admin

Who: Typically 1-2 people (Admin, Accountant); handles data collection and cross-team coordination

What they do:


Data collection & reporting: Opening cash, stock status (warehouse + vehicles), sales reports
Order management: Sales order intake from outlets, bulk orders, last orders
Billing & invoicing: Create invoices for sales, manage rough bills and formal invoices
Accounts: Maintain ledger of cash/cheque/credit transactions, track outstanding balances
Day opening/closing: Update opening cash, previous day reconciliation, update closing stock and cash
Notifications: Alert warehouse/logistics about stock levels, alert sales team/suppliers about inventory needs


What they see:


Dashboard with current stock levels (warehouse + all vehicles)
Sales order list and order history
Daily cash position and payment ledger
Outstanding credit customer list
Closing stock summary for each vehicle


Key pain point: Manual coordination between teams. If warehouse loading doesn't match sales orders, or if a vehicle returns partially loaded stock, reconciliation becomes chaotic. Currently relies on phone calls and WhatsApp messages.


Warehouse Manager / Staff

Who: 1 warehouse staff member per location (2 warehouses)

What they do:


Maintain opening stock (beginning of day)
Load products to vehicles based on sales orders
Receive returned products from vehicles (unsold stock, expired goods, damaged items)
Track closing stock at end of day
Report stock status to office


What they see:


Current warehouse stock levels by product
Load-out list for each vehicle (what to load, quantities)
Load-in list (products returned from vehicles)
Opening and closing stock summary


Key pain point: Mismatches between what's supposed to load and what's actually loaded. Stock counts don't match between office records and physical inventory.


Logistics / Service Team

Who: 1-3 people per vehicle (driver + salesperson/service staff)

What they do:


Start day with vehicle opening stock
Load products from warehouse (confirmed by office orders)
Visit outlets in planned sequence
Record delivery, collect payment
Handle returns (unsold stock, expired goods, broken items)
Report daily closing stock and revenue
Submit bills, payment details, and next-day orders


What they see:


Daily sales order list (outlets to visit, order details, delivery address)
Vehicle opening stock and current load
Outlet information (location, contact, delivery notes)
Payment collection form (cash, cheque, UPI reference)
Delivery checklist (mark delivered, note issues)
Vehicle closing stock form
Daily revenue and payment summary


Key pain point: Offline operation (poor connectivity in some areas). Current process is manual: paper invoices, WhatsApp confirmations, handwritten notes. Reconciliation at end of day is error-prone.


Accounts / Finance (Optional expanded role)

Who: If scaling, dedicated accountant for ledger and payments

What they do:


Maintain ledger of all transactions (cash, cheque, online, credit)
Track credit customers (who owes what, payment dates)
Follow up on overdue credit customers
Deposit daily cash/cheques to bank
Track fixed expenses (rent, wages, vehicle maintenance, fuel, advances, other costs)


What they see:


Ledger with all transactions (sales, payments, expenses)
Credit customer list with outstanding balances
Daily expense tracking
Bank deposit records



Sales / Order Coordination (Optional expanded role)

Who: If scaling, dedicated person for customer relationships and special orders

What they do:


Manage customer relationships with outlets
Take new orders or special requests
Ensure high stock = more sales orders, low stock = purchase from supplier
Coordinate with suppliers



3. Daily Workflow Timeline

Phase 1: Pre-Work / Preparation | 8:00 AM – 10:00 AM

Trigger: Start of business day

Actors: Office (Admin, Accountant), Warehouse, Logistics (preparing vehicles)

Activities:


Office opens day:

Update opening cash (count physical cash from previous day's vehicle returns)
Reconcile previous day invoices (what was ordered yesterday but not delivered due to outlet closed, cancelled order, or planned for today)
Review outstanding stock return reports from vehicles



Warehouse receives opening stock:

Physical count of opening stock
Load returns from previous day (unsold stock, expired goods, broken items)
Adjust stock for returns/adjustments



Office creates sales orders:

Parle Agro orders: 8:00 AM
Retail orders: 9:00 AM
Last-minute/bulk orders: 10:00 AM
Check stock levels for each product; alert warehouse if stock is low



Warehouse loads vehicles:

Receive sales order list for each vehicle
Load products into vehicle according to orders (quantity, product type)
Confirm load-out (what was actually loaded) to office
Update warehouse stock after load-out



Logistics team prepares:

Collect vehicle opening stock (what's in the vehicle at start of day)
Collect sales order and invoice list for the day
Load-in any new products from warehouse
Confirm vehicle load and departure





Data Flow:


Input: Previous day closing stock, previous day returns, today's customer orders
Output: Load-out list (confirmed), vehicle opening stock, sales invoice list


Success Criteria:


By 10:00 AM, all vehicles have left with confirmed stock and sales order lists
Office has reconciled previous day invoices and stock
No mismatches between office load-out and vehicle opening stock



Phase 2: Active / Sales & Delivery | 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM

Trigger: Vehicles leave warehouse with orders

Actors: Logistics (field teams), Office (order updates and billing)

Activities:


Logistics team delivers & collects:

Drive to outlet location (using offline customer list with addresses)
Present order and invoice, confirm delivery
Collect payment (cash, cheque, UPI, or credit/outstanding balance tracking)
Record delivery status in vehicle app (or offline notes, sync later)
Handle issues (customer wants different quantity, product expired, etc.)



Office processes incoming orders:

Receive new orders from salesman, company, outlets, walk-in customers
Create sales order for new requests
Log delivery orders (if delivered by supplier) into warehouse purchase orders
Create invoices for orders (formal or rough bill) in Tally
Update credit/cheque list as payments come in via WhatsApp or calls





Data Flow:


Input: Daily customer visits, payment collections, returns
Output: Delivery confirmations, invoices, payment records, next-day order insights


Success Criteria:


All outlets visited according to plan (or documented reason for skip)
All payments collected and reported to office
All unsold stock returned and logged
Office has updated ledger with same-day transactions



Phase 3: Post-Work / Reconciliation | 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM

Trigger: Vehicles return from field

Actors: Logistics team, Office, Warehouse

Activities:


Logistics team closes vehicle:

Return to warehouse with closing stock (unsold products)
Submit cash and cheques to office
Submit sales bills and payment receipts
Report daily sales total and vehicle closing stock
Report any undelivered invoices (customer closed, cancelled order)
Load-in (return) unsold stock to warehouse
Load-in new products for next-day sales



Warehouse receives returns:

Receive load-in (returned products from vehicle)
Check for expired, broken, or unsaleable items
Adjust warehouse closing stock



Office reconciles day:

Collect cash and cheques from logistics team
Update ledger with all sales and payments
Calculate daily sales report
Get closing stock from warehouse and all vehicles
Match invoices with payments
Identify credit customers and outstanding balances
Submit cash and cheques for bank deposit
Update closing cash in system



Day-end reporting:

If remaining stock is high: Alert sales team and suppliers to encourage more orders
If remaining stock is low: Alert supplier to purchase more inventory
Prepare next-day sales order list and vehicle load plan





Data Flow:


Input: Vehicle closing stock, cash/cheque payments, sales bills, undelivered invoices
Output: Daily sales report, closing stock, payment ledger, next-day plan


Success Criteria:


All vehicles reconciled: opening stock + load-out - sales = closing stock
All cash and cheques received and deposited
Ledger updated with all transactions
Credit customers identified and tracked
Daily sales report available for next morning
Warehouse stock matches office records
Plan ready for next day (orders, vehicle loads)



4. Core Data Entities

Entity: Inventory / Product

Primary Fields:


SKU ID (unique identifier)
SKU (product code/reference)
Brand (Bailey, Parle, Bisleri, Reliance, Masti, Sahyadri, etc.)
Brand Category (Water, Juice, Fizzy, Smoothie)
Packaging (PET Bottle, Tetra Pack)
Net Quantity (60 ml, 200 ml, 500 ml, 1 ltr, 2 ltr, 5 ltr, etc.)
MRP (Maximum Retail Price, per piece)
Case Pack (number of pieces per case/box)
Offer Pack (free pieces included in offer, if any)
GST Percent (tax rate for each product)
Purchase Rate per Case (cost to buy)
Standard Sell Rate (standard selling price)
Total Purchased Quantity / Amount (lifetime stats)
Total Sale Quantity / Amount (lifetime stats)
Adjusted Stock (returns, expired, broken items)


Relationships:


Links to Warehouse Stock (how many units in warehouse 1, warehouse 2)
Links to Vehicle Stock (how many units in Rajgad, Sinhgad, Purandar)
Links to Sales Line Items (which orders contain this product)


Validation Rules:


MRP must be ≥ Purchase Rate (profit margin check)
GST Percent must be valid tax percentage (0%, 5%, 12%, 18%)
Case Pack must be positive integer
Net Quantity must match standard sizes in system


Ownership: Admin/Manager creates; Accountant updates pricing/rates

Access: Office (view all), Warehouse (view stock levels), Logistics (view for sales)

Volume: 20-30 SKUs per brand; 50+ total SKUs; updated daily for stock and sales


Entity: Outlet / Customer

Primary Fields:


Outlet ID (unique identifier)
Outlet Name (shop/store name)
Customer Name (owner/contact person)
Contact Number (primary phone)
Postal Address (delivery address)
Email ID
Google Maps Location (latitude/longitude or map link)
GST Number (if registered)
FSSAI Number (food safety license, if applicable)
Shop Act / Udyam Number (business registration)
Outlet Picture (photo for reference)
Customer Type (Retailer, Distributor, Wholesaler, Walk-in)
Credit Limit (max outstanding balance allowed)
Current Outstanding Balance (total owed)


Relationships:


Links to Sales Orders (orders placed by this outlet)
Links to Invoices (bills generated for this outlet)
Links to Payments (cash, cheque, credit records)


Validation Rules:


Contact Number must be valid phone format
Outstanding Balance ≤ Credit Limit
Customer Type must be one of predefined list


Ownership: Admin/Manager creates; Sales team updates contact info

Access: Office (view all, create/edit), Logistics (view for delivery, update contact)

Volume: 50-100+ outlets in service area; updated weekly


Entity: Sales Order

Primary Fields:


Sales Order ID (unique, sequential or timestamp-based)
Order Date
Outlet / Customer (reference to Outlet entity)
Sales Order Status (pending, delivered, cancelled, partial)
Delivery Date (when it should be delivered)
Line Items:

Product (SKU reference)
Quantity Ordered
Unit Price
Total Amount
Discount (if any)



Payment Terms (Cash, Cheque, Credit, UPI)
Special Notes (outlet request, delivery instructions, etc.)
Created By (which team/person)
Assigned Vehicle (Rajgad, Sinhgad, Purandar, or TBD)


Relationships:


Links to Outlet (customer)
Links to Invoice (billing)
Links to Payments (cash, cheque, credit records)
Links to Inventory (products ordered)


Validation Rules:


Quantity > 0 for each line item
Unit Price ≥ 0 (allow free items for promos)
Total Amount = sum of (Quantity × Unit Price) - Discount
Outlet must exist in system
Product must exist in inventory


Ownership: Office creates

Access: Office (full access), Warehouse (view to load), Logistics (view to deliver)

Volume: 10-30 orders per day


Entity: Invoice / Billing

Primary Fields:


Invoice ID (unique, sequential)
Invoice Date
Sales Order Reference (link to SO)
Outlet / Customer (reference to Outlet)
Line Items (same as SO: product, qty, price, total, discount)
Invoice Total
Tax (GST calculation)
Grand Total (with tax)
Invoice Status (issued, delivered, paid, partially paid, cancelled)
Payment Status (unpaid, paid, cheque pending, credit)
Created Date
Delivery Date (when delivered)
Remarks (any issues, changes from SO)


Relationships:


Links to Sales Order
Links to Outlet
Links to Payments
Links to Inventory (for returns/adjustments)


Validation Rules:


Grand Total > 0
Invoice Date ≤ Delivery Date
Tax calculation based on product GST rates
Payment Status consistent with payments linked


Ownership: Office creates; Logistics updates with delivery confirmation

Access: Office (full access), Accounts (view), Logistics (view and confirm delivery)

Volume: 20-40 invoices per day


Entity: Payment / Transaction

Primary Fields:


Transaction ID (unique)
Transaction Date
Outlet / Customer (reference)
Invoice Reference (which invoice paid)
Amount
Payment Mode (Cash, Cheque, UPI, Credit/Outstanding)
If Cheque: Cheque Number, Cheque Date, Bank Name
If UPI: UPI Reference Number, Transaction ID
If Credit: Due Date for payment
Received By (which team member)
Status (Received, Pending, Bounced, Deposited)
Deposited Date (when deposited to bank, if applicable)


Relationships:


Links to Invoice
Links to Outlet


Validation Rules:


Amount > 0
Payment Mode is one of predefined list
If Cheque: Cheque Number is unique and future-dated
If UPI: Reference format valid
Status transition logical (Received → Deposited, Pending → Bounced)


Ownership: Logistics collects; Office records

Access: Office (full access), Accounts (view all, update status), Logistics (submit)

Volume: 20-40 transactions per day


Entity: Stock / Inventory Adjustments

Primary Fields:


Adjustment ID (unique)
Adjustment Date
Product (SKU reference)
Warehouse / Vehicle (which location)
Adjustment Type (Return-Expired, Return-Broken, Return-Unsold, Damaged, Shrinkage, etc.)
Quantity
Reason / Notes
Adjusted By (which team member)


Relationships:


Links to Product
Links to Invoice (if related to a return)


Validation Rules:


Quantity > 0
Product must exist
Adjustment Type is one of predefined list
Reason provided for audit trail


Ownership: Logistics submits; Office/Warehouse records

Access: Office (view all), Warehouse (submit), Logistics (submit returns)

Volume: 5-20 adjustments per day


Entity: Daily Report / Summary

Primary Fields:


Report Date
Total Sales (quantity and amount)
Total Revenue (by payment mode: cash, cheque, UPI, credit)
Total Expenses (rent, wages, fuel, vehicle maintenance, advances, other)
Net Profit / Loss
Opening Stock (by warehouse and vehicle)
Closing Stock (by warehouse and vehicle)
Stock Variance (difference between recorded and physical)
Outlets Visited
Outlets Skipped (reason)
Orders Fulfilled vs. Pending
Credit Customers (who owes, how much)
Bank Deposits (cash/cheque sent to bank)


Relationships:


Aggregates from Sales Orders, Invoices, Payments, Inventory Adjustments


Validation Rules:


All daily transactions must be included
Stock reconciliation must balance: Opening + Purchases - Sales - Returns = Closing


Ownership: Office generates

Access: Office (generate, view), Manager (view for decisions)

Volume: 1 per day


5. UI Structure & Flow

Office Team

Screen 1: Dashboard / Daily Summary

When they use it: First thing in morning and throughout the day

What it shows:


Opening cash (current day)
Stock levels: Warehouse 1, Warehouse 2, Vehicle 1 (Rajgad), Vehicle 2 (Sinhgad), Vehicle 3 (Purandar)
Today's sales (so far): Orders count, revenue, payment breakdown
Alerts: Low stock products, outstanding credit customers, undelivered orders


What they do:


View current status at a glance
Click on any section to drill down (e.g., "Low Stock" → Product list)
Navigate to order entry, reconciliation, or ledger screens


Success UX: Dashboard loads in 1-2 seconds, shows status without needing to click. Color-coded alerts (green = healthy, yellow = warning, red = urgent).


Screen 2: Sales Order Entry

When they use it: 8-10 AM, when orders arrive from outlets, company, or salespeople

What it shows:


New order form with fields: Outlet (dropdown), Products (checklist or quick-add), Quantities, Notes
Recent outlets (quick access)
Current stock levels (show available qty for each product)


What they do:


Select outlet
Add products and quantities (quick entry, multiple products per order)
Set delivery date and payment terms
Submit order to warehouse and vehicle assignment


Success UX: 5-10 seconds to create an order. Auto-complete outlet names. Quick stock check before submitting (warn if ordering out of stock).


Screen 3: Warehouse Stock Management

When they use it: Morning (for opening stock), during day (for load-out confirmation), evening (for closing stock)

What it shows:


Warehouse 1 & Warehouse 2 stock levels by product
Load-out requests for vehicles (what needs to be loaded)
Load-in returns (products coming back)
Opening and closing stock counts


What they do:


View current stock
Confirm load-out to warehouse staff
Review load-in returns and adjust stock
Update closing stock at end of day


Success UX: Real-time updates. If warehouse updates stock, office sees it immediately.


Screen 4: Vehicle Assignments & Daily Load Plan

When they use it: Morning (assign orders to vehicles), evening (review vehicle performance)

What it shows:


Sales orders for the day (sorted by outlet location or vehicle assignment)
Suggested vehicle assignment (based on location, capacity, existing load)
Vehicle opening stock and load
Vehicle routes (outlet addresses for nav)


What they do:


Assign orders to vehicles
Review load plan before vehicles leave
Adjust if needed (e.g., move order to different vehicle)
View vehicle performance (orders delivered, revenue, returns)


Success UX: Drag-and-drop to assign orders to vehicles. Show map view of outlets and vehicle routes.


Screen 5: Daily Reconciliation / Cash & Payments

When they use it: 5-8 PM, after vehicles return

What it shows:


Cash received (from all vehicles, amount, source)
Cheques received (cheque number, amount, date)
UPI/Online payments (reference, amount)
Credit/Outstanding balances (who owes, how much)
Expected vs. actual reconciliation


What they do:


Match vehicle reports with office records
Record deposits (cash and cheques to bank)
Update credit customer list
Flag discrepancies (e.g., reported payment not recorded)


Success UX: One-click deposit, automatic reconciliation. Flag mismatches for investigation.


Screen 6: Ledger / Transaction History

When they use it: Ongoing (audit trail, credit tracking), end of day (summary)

What it shows:


All transactions (date, outlet, amount, payment mode, status)
Filters: By outlet, by payment mode, by date range
Credit customers with outstanding balances and due dates
Payment history per outlet


What they do:


Search/filter transactions
View payment history
Follow up on overdue credit customers
Generate ledger reports


Success UX: Fast search, easy filtering. Show payment status (pending, received, deposited).


Screen 7: End-of-Day Report

When they use it: 7-8 PM, before closing

What it shows:


Daily summary: Total sales, revenue by mode (cash, cheque, UPI, credit), expenses
Stock reconciliation: Opening + Purchases - Sales - Returns = Closing (verify math)
Undelivered orders (outlet closed, cancelled, or will deliver tomorrow)
Credit customers and amounts
Next-day alerts (low stock, high-priority orders)


What they do:


Review summary
Approve closing stock and cash counts
Identify next-day actions
Export/print report if needed


Success UX: Auto-calculated, one-click export to email or PDF.


Warehouse Team

Screen 1: Warehouse Dashboard

When they use it: Morning (receive stock), mid-day (monitor), evening (closing)

What it shows:


Current opening stock
Load-out requests (vehicles waiting for stock)
Load-in returns (products coming back)
Stock level by product


What they do:


View stock
Confirm receipt of new stock (from supplier purchase order)
Load products to vehicles per request
Receive and log returns


Success UX: Clear list of what to load and when. One-click confirmation.


Screen 2: Load-Out Fulfillment

When they use it: Morning / pre-departure (when vehicles are loading)

What it shows:


Load-out list by vehicle: Product, Quantity to load
Current warehouse stock (verify availability)
Confirmation checklist (loaded item by item)


What they do:


Pick products from warehouse
Load into vehicle
Scan/confirm each item loaded (or manual checkmark)
Submit load-out confirmation to office


Success UX: Barcode scan or manual entry. Clear checkboxes for each item. Prevents loading without confirmation.


Screen 3: Load-In / Returns

When they use it: Evening (when vehicles return)

What it shows:


Returned products from vehicle (unsold, expired, broken)
Warehouse current stock
Options to categorize returns (Unsold, Expired, Broken, Damaged)


What they do:


Receive products from vehicle
Categorize returns
Update warehouse closing stock
Log adjustment (for audit trail)


Success UX: Simple checklist. Option to take photo if damaged/expired (for audit).


Logistics / Service Team (Field)

Screen 1: Daily Orders & Route Plan

When they use it: Morning (before leaving), mid-day (reference during deliveries)

What it shows:


Assigned sales orders (outlet name, address, products, quantities)
Route (map with outlet locations, suggested order)
Delivery status (pending, delivered, skipped, partial)
Outstanding balance (if customer owes credit)


What they do:


Review orders and route
Navigate to outlets
Update status as delivered


Success UX: Offline-available map and order list. Touch-friendly for phone use. One-tap to mark delivered.


Screen 2: Delivery Confirmation & Payment Collection

When they use it: At each outlet (3-5 minutes per stop)

What it shows:


Outlet name, address, contact
Invoice (products, quantities, amount)
Payment form with options: Cash, Cheque, UPI, Credit/Outstanding
Signature or photo confirmation (optional)


What they do:


Present invoice to outlet
Collect payment (cash, cheque, UPI link, or record as credit)
Take signature or photo if needed
Record delivery


Success UX: Mobile-first. One screen per outlet. Fast payment entry (cash field + submit). Offline mode: record payment, sync when online.


Screen 3: Returns / Stock Adjustment

When they use it: During deliveries (if outlet rejects product or returns old stock)

What it shows:


Return type options: Expired, Broken, Unsold, Damaged, Other
Product and quantity
Notes/photo


What they do:


Record return reason
Adjust load stock
Note issue for office follow-up


Success UX: Simple categorization. Photo option for audit trail.


Screen 4: Vehicle Opening & Closing Stock

When they use it: Morning (after warehouse load-out), evening (before return)

What it shows:


Vehicle opening stock (what was loaded)
Current vehicle inventory (products in vehicle, quantities)
Closing stock (what remains at end of day)
Variance check (does it match?)


What they do:


Confirm opening stock (received from warehouse)
Track stock during day (load updates as orders placed)
Report closing stock (unsold, to be returned to warehouse)
Sync closing stock with office


Success UX: Auto-calculated based on sales. Offline support: record locally, sync at warehouse return.


Screen 5: Daily Revenue & Payment Summary

When they use it: End of day (before returning to warehouse)

What it shows:


Total sales (quantity and amount)
Payments received (cash, cheque, UPI, credit customers)
Cash total (ready to submit)
Any issues or notes


What they do:


Review day's performance
Submit cash and cheques to office
Report any discrepancies


Success UX: Auto-summed from deliveries. Clear breakdown by payment mode. One-click to submit.


Screen 6: Next-Day Order Planning

When they use it: Evening (planning tomorrow)

What it shows:


Standing orders (regular customers)
Special requests
Suggested load based on opening stock


What they do:


Confirm tomorrow's orders
Note any changes or new requests
Submit to office for next-day load plan


Success UX: Prefilled with regular orders. Easy to add/remove. Submit once to office.


6. UI/UX Principles

Design Priorities


Role Isolation: Each team sees only relevant data

Office: Orders, stock, payments, ledger
Warehouse: Stock, load-out, returns
Logistics: Orders, route, customer data, payment collection
No cross-team confusion or access to unnecessary data



Real-Time Sync: Instant feedback when critical actions happen

Vehicle loads stock → Office sees it immediately
Outlet pays in cash → Logistics confirms, office receives update
Warehouse logs return → Stock adjusted in real-time
Builds trust in the system



Speed Over Perfection: Field teams move fast

Delivery confirmation should take 10-30 seconds per outlet
Order entry should take 30 seconds for typical order
No unnecessary clicks, no long forms
Sacrifice animations and polish for responsiveness



Offline-First for Field: Logistics team loses connectivity

App works offline: all orders, routes, customer info cached
Payment recorded offline, synced when online
No delays, no data loss
Queue syncs automatically when connection restored



Mobile-Optimized for Field, Desktop for Office

Logistics: Phone-first design (responsive, touch-friendly, large buttons)
Office: Desktop design (wider screens, multi-window workflows, bulk entry)
Adaptive design: App detects device and optimizes layout



Audit Trail & Accountability: Every transaction logged

Timestamp on every action
User/team recorded
Reason for adjustments
Photo or signature for high-value items
Enables reconciliation and reduces disputes



Data Validation & Consistency: Prevent human error

Warn if stock would go negative
Warn if order exceeds credit limit
Match expected vs. actual reconciliation
Flag discrepancies immediately





Visual & Interaction Patterns


Status Colors: Green (delivered, paid), Yellow (pending, warning), Red (issue, overdue)
Navigation: Bottom tab bar for mobile (Orders, Delivery, Stock, Revenue). Sidebar for desktop (Dashboard, Orders, Stock, Ledger, Reports)
Quick Actions: Floating button for common tasks (New Order, Mark Delivered, Record Payment)
Summaries: Large, bold numbers for key metrics (Total Sales, Cash Collected, Stock Alert)
Forms: Minimal friction (auto-fill, dropdowns, one-field-per-line on mobile)
Maps: Show outlets as pins; tap for address, directions, and order details
Notifications: Toast alerts for critical events (Low Stock Alert, Payment Confirmed, Vehicle Returned)
Offline Indicator: Show sync status in header; icon when offline, "syncing..." when reconnected


Typography & Layout


Headlines: Bold, large (20px+) for screen titles and key metrics
Body text: 14-16px for comfortable reading on phone and desktop
Form labels: Small, gray, above input field
Spacing: Generous padding (16px+) for touch targets on mobile
Density: Office views can be denser (12px text, compact tables); Field views must be spacious


Accessibility


Touch targets: minimum 44x44 px for mobile
Color: Don't rely solely on color (use icons, labels)
Contrast: Text must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text)
Labels: All inputs labeled; form must be keyboard navigable



7. Technical Architecture Recommendations

Frontend Stack


Framework: React.js or Next.js (component reusability, real-time state updates)
Mobile: Responsive web (single codebase) OR React Native (if native app preferred)
State Management: Redux or Zustand (to sync office, warehouse, logistics data)
Offline Support: Service Worker + local storage (cache orders, routes, customer data)
Maps: Google Maps or Mapbox (display routes, outlet locations)
UI Library: Material-UI, Chakra, or custom components for consistency


Backend Stack


Framework: Node.js + Express or Python (Django/FastAPI)
Database: PostgreSQL (relational, strong ACID guarantees for inventory and payments)
Architecture: REST API (or GraphQL if complex queries needed)
APIs needed:

/orders — Create, read, update sales orders
/invoices — Billing and delivery tracking
/payments — Payment recording and ledger
/inventory — Stock levels, adjustments
/outlets — Customer data and credit limits
/reports — Daily summary generation
/sync — Offline sync for field team





Authentication & Roles


Auth: Email/password or PIN (simple, field-friendly)
Role-Based Access Control:

Office (full access to orders, inventory, payments, reports)
Warehouse (stock, load-out, returns)
Logistics (view orders, record delivery, collect payment)
Admin (user management, settings, reports)





Offline Sync Strategy


Service Worker: Cache all essential data (orders, customers, products, maps)
Local Storage: Store pending payments, deliveries, returns
Sync Queue: When online, queue uploads to server (with retry logic)
Conflict Resolution: Last-write-wins or user selects version on conflict


Performance Targets


Load time: < 2 seconds on 4G
Sync latency: < 5 seconds on WiFi, < 15 seconds on 4G
Offline sync: Batch 10+ transactions, upload within 2 minutes of reconnect
Mobile responsiveness: < 100ms for user interactions


Deployment & Hosting


Hosting: AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure (for 24/7 uptime)
Database backup: Daily backups, disaster recovery plan
API rate limiting: Prevent abuse; queue up to 100 concurrent users
Monitoring: Error tracking (Sentry), performance monitoring (Datadog or similar)


Security


HTTPS only: Encrypt all data in transit
Data encryption: Sensitive fields (payments, credit info) encrypted at rest
Audit logging: Track all transactions, edits, deletions
Access control: Role-based permissions, no cross-team data leakage
PCI compliance: If handling card payments, follow PCI-DSS standards (or use third-party payment processor)



8. Success Criteria

The application is complete and ready for deployment when:

Functional Completeness


✓ Office dashboard: Shows stock, sales, payments, alerts in real-time
✓ Order entry: Create, edit, assign orders to vehicles in < 30 seconds
✓ Warehouse load-out: Confirm products loaded per order, sync with office
✓ Delivery confirmation: Logistics team marks delivered, collects payment in < 1 minute per outlet
✓ Payment collection: Record cash, cheque, UPI, credit/outstanding in field
✓ Daily reconciliation: Office matches vehicle reports with stock and cash, identifies discrepancies
✓ Ledger & credit tracking: All transactions logged, credit customers tracked with balance and due date
✓ Daily reports: Auto-generated summary (sales, revenue, stock, expenses) by end of business day


Data Integrity


✓ Stock accuracy: Physical stock matches app records (within acceptable variance)
✓ Transaction logging: Every sale, payment, return logged with timestamp and user
✓ Reconciliation math: Opening + Purchases - Sales - Returns = Closing Stock (verified daily)
✓ Payment matching: Each invoice linked to payment (cash, cheque, credit, or pending)
✓ Audit trail: All edits, deletions, adjustments tracked (who, when, why)


Performance & Reliability


✓ Offline functionality: Logistics team can work without internet (8+ hours), no data loss on sync
✓ Response time: Dashboard loads < 2 seconds, forms submit < 1 second
✓ Uptime: 99% availability (max 7 hours downtime per month)
✓ Backup: Daily automated backups, disaster recovery tested
✓ Sync accuracy: All offline transactions sync correctly when online


UI/UX Quality


✓ Mobile usability: Field team can complete delivery in < 1 minute per outlet on phone
✓ Office efficiency: Order entry, reconciliation, and reporting are faster than manual process
✓ Accessibility: Large touch targets (44x44 px), readable text (14px+), clear labels
✓ Intuitiveness: New user can navigate main workflows without training (after brief intro)
✓ Error prevention: Warnings for out-of-stock, credit limit exceeded, missing fields


Business Outcomes


✓ Time savings: Daily reconciliation takes < 30 minutes (vs. 1+ hour manual)
✓ Accuracy: Stock variances reduced to < 2% (vs. 5-10% with manual)
✓ Cash collection: No cash discrepancies (all payments tracked in app)
✓ Credit management: Overdue credits reduced through better tracking and follow-up
✓ Visibility: Manager can view full business status (orders, revenue, stock, payments) in 5 minutes


Stakeholder Sign-Off


✓ Office team: Confirms app simplifies their workflow and reduces errors
✓ Warehouse team: Confirms load-out and stock tracking are accurate and fast
✓ Logistics team: Confirms delivery and payment collection are quick and reliable
✓ Manager: Approves daily reports, performance metrics, and overall system
✓ Accountant: Confirms ledger, credit tracking, and audit trail meet requirements
